game_name: Space Quest III: The Pirates of Pestulon

danger_signals.meaning: Sinister antagonist faction controlling the main threat; visual marker of danger zone

danger_signals.visual_form: Combat arena designed for forced mecha robot battles
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat of forced combat; player is trapped and in danger from Elmo's control

danger_signals.visual_form: Fortified installation on hostile volcanic terrain
danger_signals.meaning: Defensive military structure indicating restricted/dangerous zone requiring neutralization

danger_signals.visual_form: Humanoid mechanical/android form with terminatror aesthetic
danger_signals.meaning: Relentless pursuer; visual embodiment of past consequences catching up to player

safety_signals.meaning: Neutral trading hub; relatively safe zone for information gathering
Player gains stealth capability; visual representation of player empowerment for safe infiltration
Camouflage within enemy territory; visual signal of blending in and reduced danger

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: ScumSoft corporate branding on Planet Pestulon
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Elmo Pug as faction leader; ScumSoft space ships as faction military assets
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Pestulon planet base; cubicle-based company headquarters with whip-cracking overseers; arena battle facility
Earth headquarters where Ken Williams resides; destination for rescued programmers; represents legitimate game development vs. corporate exploitation

faction_indicators.faction: The Two Guys from Andromeda (protagonist allies)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Hidden distress message embedded in arcade game
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Imprisoned within ScumSoft facility; rescue objective indicates player alliance

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Escape pod drifting through space; garbage freighter backdrop
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Awakening triggers exploration/survival mode; player must navigate garbage hold hazards

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Broken-down vessel in garbage hold requiring repair
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player transitions from passive survival to active problem-solving; ship repair mechanics unlock escape path
Terminator encounter forces combat/evasion; obtaining invisibility belt changes player capability state

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Corporate headquarters on Pestulon; military presence (shield generator); arena structures
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: World shifts from exploration to infiltration; player must employ stealth and disguise mechanics
Planetary defenses compromised; player gains access to surface infiltration

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Space combat against multiple ScumSoft ships; warp drive malfunction
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Transition from stealth to action; emergence into parallel dimension via black hole indicates world transformation

environmental_storytelling.technique: Sci-fi parody wreckage in garbage freighter
environmental_storytelling.example: TIE fighter, ACME Rocket, Jupiter 2 spacecraft debris communicates world as parody of known franchises; establishes tone of comedic homage to Star Trek/Star Wars

ScumSoft headquarters depicting overseers with whips cracking over cubicled developers; satirizes real-world corporate exploitation and communicates evil corporate faction through workplace visual metaphor
Hidden distress message within Astro Chicken minigame reveals Two Guys' imprisonment; game-within-game mechanic mirrors the plot of rescuing programmers, creating narrative coherence
Planet Pestulon as ScumSoft stronghold; lava moon as militarized shield location; orbital Monolith Burger as neutral/commercial space all communicate faction control and world political boundaries

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Awakens in cryogenic pod in garbage hold; must repair derelict ship to escape; demonstrates low power/agency at start
After obtaining invisibility belt and disguise, infiltrates enemy base; demonstrates increased agency and stealth capability
Confronts Roger on Phleebhut to enforce past debt; relentless mechanical enemy representing consequence mechanics
Traps Roger in arena; forces mecha robot combat; represents final confrontation mechanic before escape
Pursue Roger during escape sequence with space ships; demonstrate faction military capability and escalating threat as player approaches objective completion

notes: Space Quest III uses environmental and faction visual design to communicate a parody universe where established sci-fi is repurposed for narrative humor. The core danger/safety distinction relies on location aesthetics (corporate=hostile, station=neutral) and equipment acquisition (invisibility, disguise). World state transitions are marked by location changes and equipment availability rather than dynamic environmental shifts. The game's satirical tone is reinforced through visual juxtaposition (corporate whip-cracking overseer parody) and nostalgic wreckage references that communicate genre awareness. Political situation is simplified: ScumSoft (evil corporation) vs. legitimate game industry (Sierra), with protagonist as underdog infiltrator.
